message.content:
POLARIS MISSION (this wheel): Make the OUTLINE stage climb RACE on the CORRECTED corpus — by fixing GENERAL quality bugs, NOT by tuning to any benchmark task (that overfitting was the operator-flagged mistake). Input corpus (metadata now correct, sources properly tiered/deduped): /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json (DOI 243, journal 287, UNKNOWN 145, 87 dupes flagged). RACE gap diagnosis (measured): our reports lose most on INSIGHT (2.97x behind, weight 0.32) and COMPREHENSIVENESS (2.43x) because the outline structure is WRONG and SHALLOW — it emits CLINICAL-TRIAL template section headers (Efficacy/Safety/Comparative/Long-term Outcomes) even on non-clinical topics like AI-and-labor, with no intro, no thematic synthesis sections, no conclusion/research-gaps. That is a GENERAL structural bug that hurts EVERY query, not just one benchmark.
SUCCESS IS MEASURED BY: (1) cp4_used=agentic on the corrected corpus, full corpus, never fallback-plain. (2) The section structure is TOPIC-APPROPRIATE for the actual research question (NOT the clinical template on non-clinical topics) and is synthesis-enabling: intro/framing, thematic sections that group related evidence, a cross-study synthesis/contradictions section, conclusion + research-gaps. (3) GENERALITY PROVEN: the structure fix works across >=3 DIFFERENT-DOMAIN questions (e.g. an AI/labor, a medical, a finance question), NOT tuned to one. (4) Re-scored on RACE (score_report_race.py, gpt-5.5 judge) the outline-driven report's COMPREHENSIVENESS and INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING improve vs the 0.30 best-compose baseline. Faithfulness gate stays hard: no derived/unverified number renders via [CITE:ev_xxx].
The bar is not "bug fixed" — it is the benchmark number at the top, VERIFIED. Keep going until the
measurement says top (or you hit a hard external blocker you must report honestly).

FENCE (military discipline — no exceptions):
- Work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (branch bot/outline-agent-box). NEVER touch another worktree.
- NEVER edit anything under /home/polaris/polaris_project (operator's monitoring dir — deck.sh, wheel*.js, wheels.py).
  If you catch yourself editing a monitoring script or a workflow .js, you have DRIFTED — stop.
- Report REAL output only. Never invent, never claim a pass you did not observe, never fake a benchmark number.
- Commit every landed step in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent; append one dated line to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md.


YOU ARE FABLE — the scalpel. You are NOT here to read or search the codebase; Opus already did the
hard reading. You are here for ONE precise deep-think: judge what Opus did and decide the next step.

WHAT OPUS DID THIS ROUND:
{
  "did": [
    "REPRODUCED the clinical-template bug on the corrected 997-evidence AI/labor corpus: the agentic outline emitted Mechanism/Efficacy/Safety/Regulatory/Population Subgroups/Long-term Outcomes because (a) compose never threaded domain into generate_multi_section_report (defaulted '' -> clinical _ALLOWED_SECTIONS) and (b) even the non-clinical facet path was a flat bag with no intro/synthesis/conclusion. Prior STEP-16 masked it with a hardcoded clinical->AI/labor relabel MAP + hardcoded intro/title (overfit).",
    "FIXED at the source, general/structural: added DEFAULT-OFF flag PG_FACET_OUTLINE_SKELETON in multi_section_generator.py that wraps the emergent facet outline in the standard research-report skeleton (OPEN framing -> thematic bodies with EMERGENT titles -> Cross-Study Synthesis & Contradictions -> Conclusions & Research Gaps). Only the four structural ROLES are fixed; no topic/title/benchmark hardcoded. Clinical/unknown path byte-identical.",
    "DE-OVERFIT the compose harness: threaded domain=corpus.domain, deleted the _ARCHETYPE_HEADINGS relabel map + _humanize_heading + hardcoded AI/labor intro/title, added _derive_title (strips leading imperatives from RQ), render section titles verbatim, topic-neutral framing sentence.",
    "PROVED generality at outline-seed level across 3 domains (scripts/outline_generality_probe.py, one LLM call each): AI/labor, MEDICAL (gut microbiota), FINANCE (global insurers) ALL produce Introduction -> thematic bodies -> Cross-Study Synthesis/Contradictions -> Conclusions/Research Gaps, topic-appropriate, zero clinical leak.",
    "RAN the full live agentic compose over the corrected 997-evidence/329-basket corpus: cp4_used=agentic (outline agent loop, 9 react turns, search_more_evidence + update_outline), 8 topic-driven sections ALL kept, faithfulness PASS (0 leaked [CITE:ev], 0 unresolved markers). Confirmed raw multi_section_outline.json has ZERO clinical archetypes.",
    "SCORED on RACE task 72 (gpt-5.5 judge) and beat the 0.30 best-compose baseline on every dimension."
  ],
  "commits": [
    "1cf3308",
    "f1ecf14"
  ],
  "files": [
    "src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py",
    "scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py",
    "scripts/outline_generality_probe.py",
    "WHEEL_PROGRESS.md",
    "outputs/step2_topic_structure/report.md",
    "outputs/outline_generality_probe.json"
  ],
  "exercised": "Outline generality: ran scripts/outline_generality_probe.py live (real deepseek-v4-pro seed call) on 3 domains — printed the actual section plans (all topic-appropriate, no archetypes). Full pipeline: ran scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py live (PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1, GLM-5.2) over the corrected 997-evidence corpus for ~30min -> real report.md with 8 topic-driven sections, cp4_used=agentic confirmed in logs (outline_agent update_outline/search_more_evidence turns). RACE: ran the official third_party/deep_research_bench RACE harness (gpt-5.5) end-to-end -> real race_result.txt. Not py_compile — real LLM runs and a real judged benchmark number.",
  "measurement": "RACE task 72, gpt-5.5 judge, on outputs/step2_topic_structure/report.md (obtained via scripts/score_report_race.py, log outputs/step2_race_score.log): Comprehensiveness 0.4435 (baseline 0.3391, +0.104), Insight 0.4192 (0.2875, +0.132), Instruction Following 0.4389 (0.2775, +0.161), Readability 0.4245 (0.2966, +0.128), OVERALL 0.4317 (best-compose 0.3023, +0.129). Beats the 0.30 baseline on all four dimensions and edges the claude-3-7-sonnet reference (0.4218 overall) sitting in the results dir. Faithfulness gate PASS (leaked_[CITE:ev]=0). Generality: outputs/outline_generality_probe.json shows the same intro/thematic/synthesis-contradictions/conclusion-gaps skeleton on AI/labor + medical + finance.",
  "open": [
    "Generality was proven at the OUTLINE-SEED level for medical/finance (one LLM call each), but only AI/labor got a full compose+RACE number (the other two DRB tasks have no pre-built corpus; building one needs the retrieval stack).",
    "Minor cosmetic artifact in the rendered prose: adjacent citation markers occasionally render with a stray period ('[6].[7]'); pre-existing sentence-assembly behavior, not a faithfulness issue; did not chase this round.",
    "0.4317 beats the stated 0.30 baseline and the sonnet reference, but true deep-research SOTA on RACE is higher (~0.45-0.50 overall); more headroom likely exists in per-section depth/insight."
  ],
  "status": "PROGRESS",
  "blockers": []
}

OPUS'S PRECISE QUESTION FOR YOU:
Is the outline-seed-level generality proof on medical+finance (plus a full compose+RACE only on AI/labor, overall 0.4317 vs 0.30 baseline, +0.13 on every dimension, faithfulness PASS) sufficient to call the topic-driven structure fix DONE and generalized — or does the bar require a full compose+RACE on a SECOND-domain corpus (which means building a new corpus via the retrieval stack, a much larger lift), before we move on to squeezing more Insight depth?



DECIDE, precisely:
1. at_top: does the REAL measurement show top benchmark performance? ((1) cp4_used=agentic on the corrected corpus, full corpus, never fallback-plain. (2) The section structure is TOPIC-APPROPRIATE for the actual research question (NOT the clinical template on non-clinical topics) and is synthesis-enabling: intro/framing, thematic sections that group related evidence, a cross-study synthesis/contradictions section, conclusion + research-gaps. (3) GENERALITY PROVEN: the structure fix works across >=3 DIFFERENT-DOMAIN questions (e.g. an AI/labor, a medical, a finance question), NOT tuned to one. (4) Re-scored on RACE (score_report_race.py, gpt-5.5 judge) the outline-driven report's COMPREHENSIVENESS and INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING improve vs the 0.30 best-compose baseline. Faithfulness gate stays hard: no derived/unverified number renders via [CITE:ev_xxx].)
2. correct: is the diff correct for what it claims?
3. faithfulness_safe: are the invariants intact (no derived/unverified number can render; fold-in seam intact)?
4. verdict: SIGN_OFF only if at_top AND correct AND faithfulness_safe. Else CONTINUE (or FIX_REGRESSION if
   Opus broke something). NEVER force-approve. A measurement Opus could not take is NOT a sign-off.
5. next_step: the single most important next action for Opus — precise, file:line where you can.
Think deeply here; this is the one place the wheel spends deep reasoning. Keep it tight and decisive.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

commit 1cf3308ad8160ef2ce652866d6bc9ddcecd43cb4
Author: polaris-bot <bot@polaris>
Date:   Sun Jul 12 01:40:31 2026 +0000

    STEP 2 (topic-driven structure): general research-report skeleton for facet outline + de-overfit compose
    
    ROOT CAUSE (reproduced): the agentic outline emitted CLINICAL archetype headers
    (Mechanism/Efficacy/Safety/Regulatory/...) on the non-clinical AI-labor corpus because
    (1) compose never threaded `domain` into generate_multi_section_report (defaulted "" ->
    clinical _ALLOWED_SECTIONS), and (2) even the non-clinical path was a flat facet bag with no
    intro / synthesis / conclusion. STEP-16 papered over it with a hardcoded clinical->AI/labor
    relabel MAP + hardcoded AI/labor intro/title — overfit to one benchmark task.
    
    FIX (general, structural, no topic hardcoded):
    - multi_section_generator: new DEFAULT-OFF flag PG_FACET_OUTLINE_SKELETON. When on (facet mode
      active), _select_outline_system_prompt appends _FACET_SKELETON_ADDENDUM to the facet prompt,
      wrapping the emergent thematic facets in the standard research-report skeleton: OPEN framing
      section -> thematic bodies (titles still EMERGE) -> Cross-Study Synthesis & Contradictions ->
      Conclusions & Research Gaps. Only the four structural ROLES are fixed; no topic/title/benchmark
      is hardcoded. Clinical/unknown path byte-identical (flag scoped to non-clinical facet mode).
    - compose_agentic_report_s3gear329: thread domain=corpus.domain into the generator; DELETE the
      _ARCHETYPE_HEADINGS relabel map + _humanize_heading + hardcoded AI/labor intro/title; derive a
      neutral title from the RQ (_derive_title strips leading imperatives); render section titles
      verbatim; general topic-neutral framing sentence. Sets PG_FACET_OUTLINE/SKELETON via setdefault.
    
    GENERALITY PROVEN (scripts/outline_generality_probe.py, one seed LLM call per domain):
    - AI/labor (domain=workforce): Introduction -> 4 thematic bodies -> Cross-Study Synthesis and
      Contradictions -> Conclusions and Research Gaps. No clinical archetypes.
    - MEDICAL (gut microbiota): Introduction -> 3 thematic bodies -> Cross-Study Synthesis ->
      Conclusions and Research Gaps.
    - FINANCE (global insurers): Introduction -> 4 thematic bodies -> Cross-Study Convergence and
      Contradictions -> Conclusions and Future Asset Prospects.
    All three: topic-appropriate, synthesis-enabling, zero clinical-template leak, zero tuning.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

      neutral title from the RQ (_derive_title strips leading imperatives); render section titles
      verbatim; general topic-neutral framing sentence. Sets PG_FACET_OUTLINE/SKELETON via setdefault.
    
    GENERALITY PROVEN (scripts/outline_generality_probe.py, one seed LLM call per domain):
    - AI/labor (domain=workforce): Introduction -> 4 thematic bodies -> Cross-Study Synthesis and
      Contradictions -> Conclusions and Research Gaps. No clinical archetypes.
    - MEDICAL (gut microbiota): Introduction -> 3 thematic bodies -> Cross-Study Synthesis ->
      Conclusions and Research Gaps.
    - FINANCE (global insurers): Introduction -> 4 thematic bodies -> Cross-Study Convergence and
      Contradictions -> Conclusions and Future Asset Prospects.
    All three: topic-appropriate, synthesis-enabling, zero clinical-template leak, zero tuning.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -861,6 +861,42 @@ def _facet_outline_active_for_domain(domain: str | None) -> bool:
     return str(domain or "").strip().lower() not in ("", "clinical")
 
 
+# GENERAL RESEARCH-REPORT SKELETON (mission STEP 2 — synthesis-enabling, topic-DRIVEN structure).
+# The bare facet outline emits a flat BAG of thematic sections with no intro, no cross-study
+# synthesis, and no conclusion/gaps — the exact SHALLOW-STRUCTURE defect that costs INSIGHT and
+# COMPREHENSIVENESS on RACE. This flag wraps the emergent thematic facets in the standard
+# research-report skeleton (intro/overview -> thematic bodies -> cross-study synthesis &
+# contradictions -> conclusions & research gaps). It is a GENERAL structural improvement that
+# applies to ANY non-clinical topic (AI/labor, finance, ecology, ...); it hardcodes NO topic,
+# NO benchmark prompt, and NO thematic title (those still EMERGE from the evidence facets). The
+# four structural ROLES are the only fixed part — a standard scholarly report skeleton. Read at
+# CALL time (env/monkeypatch-testable). DEFAULT-OFF => the bare facet prompt (byte-identical).
+_FACET_SKELETON_ENV = "PG_FACET_OUTLINE_SKELETON"
+
+
+def _facet_skeleton_enabled() -> bool:
+    """DEFAULT-OFF flag (read at call time). ON wraps the facet outline in the general
+    research-report skeleton (intro / thematic bodies / synthesis+contradictions / conclusion+gaps).
+    Only takes effect where facet mode is already active (non-clinical); clinical stays untouched."""
+    return os.getenv(_FACET_SKELETON_ENV, "0").strip().lower() not in (
+        "0", "", "false", "no", "off",
+    )
+
+
+# The skeleton addendum appended to OUTLINE_SYSTEM_PROMPT_FACET when _facet_skeleton_enabled().
+# Structure-ONLY: it constrains the ORDER and the presence of four structural roles; the thematic
+# body TITLES and COUNT still emerge from the evidence. Topic-agnostic by construction.
+_FACET_SKELETON_ADDENDUM = """
+
+GENERAL RESEARCH-REPORT SKELETON (structure the plan as a COMPLETE report, not a flat bag of topics):
+Order the sections so the report reads as a coherent scholarly review with these FOUR structural roles present in EVERY report, regardless of topic:
+  1. OPEN with exactly ONE framing/overview section that orients the reader to the question, the scope, and the body of evidence (a good title names the subject, e.g. "Introduction and Scope of the Evidence" or "Overview of <subject>"). Assign it broad, high-level or foundational evidence.
+  2. THEN the THEMATIC BODY sections — one per distinct facet the evidence genuinely supports (as instructed above). These titles EMERGE from the evidence; there is no fixed menu and no fixed count.
+  3. Include exactly ONE cross-cutting SYNTHESIS section that reads ACROSS the thematic bodies and surfaces where studies AGREE, where they CONTRADICT each other, and how strong/consistent the overall evidence is (e.g. "Cross-Study Synthesis and Contradictions"). Assign it the evidence whose findings converge or conflict.
+  4. CLOSE with exactly ONE section covering CONCLUSIONS and open RESEARCH GAPS (e.g. "Conclusions and Research Gaps"): what the evidence collectively supports, and what remains unresolved or under-studied.
+The four ROLES (open / thematic bodies / synthesis+contradictions / conclusions+gaps) are REQUIRED; the thematic-body titles are free to emerge from the evidence. Each section — including the four structural ones — still needs at least 2 genuinely supporting evidence IDs; assign the relevant rows, never fabricate a section the evidence cannot ground."""
+
+
 def _facet_outline_max_sections() -> int:
     """Compute-safety ceiling for the facet outline (read at call time). Not a target."""
     try:
@@ -1580,6 +1616,11 @@ def _select_outline_system_prompt(domain: str | None) -> str:
     generic outline prompt (I-ready-009 #1081). O1 (#1344): when the facet-outline flag is ON for a
     non-clinical domain, use the facet-driven prompt so section titles/count emerge from the evidence."""
     if _facet_outline_active_for_domain(domain):
+        # STEP 2: when the skeleton flag is on, wrap the emergent facets in the general
+        # research-report skeleton (intro / thematic bodies / synthesis+contradictions /
+        # conclusion+gaps). Structure-only; thematic titles still emerge from the evidence.
+        if _facet_skeleton_enabled():
+            return OUTLINE_SYSTEM_PROMPT_FACET + _FACET_SKELETON_ADDENDUM
         return OUTLINE_SYSTEM_PROMPT_FACET
     return (

content.text: I have enough to decide. Confirmed the real evidence: cp4_used=agentic (live outline_agent loop with search_more_evidence + update_outline over the corrected 329-basket / 1120-row corpus), raw multi_section_outline.json has zero clinical archetypes, de-overfit is real (deleted _ARCHETYPE_HEADINGS + _humanize_heading, threaded domain, verbatim titles), RACE 0.4317 with comprehensiveness 0.4435 and instruction-following 0.4389 both beating 0.30, faithfulness PASS (0 leaked [CITE:ev], 49/128 sentences dropped at the fold-in gate = seam intact).

input.gap_to_top: The four STATED success criteria are all met and verified with real measurement: (1) cp4_used=agentic on the full corrected 329-basket/1120-row corpus, never fallback (confirmed by live outline_agent search_more_evidence/update_outline turns); (2) topic-appropriate synthesis-enabling structure (Intro/Scope -> 5 emergent thematic bodies -> Cross-Study Synthesis & Contradictions -> Conclusions & Research Gaps, zero clinical archetypes in raw outline); (3) generality proven across AI/labor, medical, finance at the outline-seed level; (4) RACE comprehensiveness 0.4435 and instruction-following 0.4389 both beat the 0.30 baseline, all four dims +0.10..+0.16, faithfulness PASS. BUT the mission bar is the number AT THE TOP, and Opus itself cites deep-research SOTA at ~0.45-0.50 overall vs our 0.4317. The lowest dimension is INSIGHT 0.4192 — which is also the highest-weight RACE lever (0.32) and the mission's originally-diagnosed biggest gap. So the structural bug is fixed and generalized, but absolute top is not yet reached; proven headroom remains in per-section insight depth.
input.reason: Nothing is broken (correct + faithfulness_safe both true), so not FIX_REGRESSION. Not SIGN_OFF because at_top is false: 0.4317 beats the baseline and the sonnet reference but sits below the ~0.45-0.50 SOTA Opus cites, and Insight (0.4192, weight 0.32) is the weakest dimension and the mission's headline gap. On Opus's actual question: the outline-seed generality probe SATISFIES criterion (3) as written ('structure fix works across >=3 different-domain questions') — and, critically, the fix is a domain-independent structural prompt addendum (_FACET_SKELETON_ADDENDUM fixes only the four roles, no topic/title hardcoded), so a second full compose+RACE corpus would add little generality confidence for a large retrieval-stack lift, and chasing a second benchmark task risks re-introducing the exact overfitting the operator flagged. Do NOT build a second-domain corpus now. Spend the next wheel on Insight depth, the real remaining lever.
input.next_step: Do NOT build a second-domain RACE corpus (large lift, marginal generality gain, overfitting risk). Attack INSIGHT depth — the highest-weight lever (0.32) and lowest dim (0.4192). Concrete: 49 of 128 candidate sentences (38%) are dropped at the faithfulness fold-in gate, starving body/synthesis sections of verified quantitative contrasts. In src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py, focus the 'Cross-Study Synthesis and Contradictions' role: ensure it is ASSIGNED the evidence rows whose verified findings converge/conflict and PROMPTED to quantify agreement/disagreement across those verified numbers (comparing the [CITE:ev]-backed figures the fold-in already accepts), rather than restating body claims. Raise verified-evidence density per thematic section WITHOUT relaxing the fold-in seam (keep leaked_[CITE:ev]=0, keep dropping unverified sentences). Then re-score on RACE task 72 and confirm Insight climbs toward 0.45-0.50; commit + append the dated WHEEL_PROGRESS.md line.
